decisions.title: Two knowledge scopes
decisions.context: Nightshift must keep reusable framework guidance separate from knowledge and control files that belong to a target project.
decisions.decision: Framework knowledge lives in the Nightshift repository. Project knowledge and all project-specific Nightshift control data live in the target project's .nightshift directory.
consequences: Nightshift can operate on unrelated repositories without embedding project-specific knowledge.
Project-specific knowledge is versioned together with the target project's code.
Only framework and project knowledge scopes are supported.

decisions.title: Simplicity first
decisions.context: Nightshift must remain understandable and evolve through small, safe steps rather than speculative abstractions.
decisions.decision: Choose the simplest implementation that satisfies the current verified need. Introduce abstractions only after repeated real use demonstrates that they are necessary.
consequences: Changes should be small and independently verifiable.
Functions and simple data structures are preferred over frameworks and indirection.
Architecture follows observed needs rather than anticipated complexity.

decisions.title: Repository responsibilities
decisions.context: The boundary between the Nightshift framework and a target project must be explicit.
decisions.decision: The Nightshift repository owns runtime behavior and framework knowledge. The target project repository owns backlog, project knowledge, architecture decisions, contracts, prompts, and project rules under .nightshift.
consequences: Adding a new target project must not require project-specific changes to Nightshift.
Project control data can be reviewed and changed in the same workflow as project code.

decisions.title: JSON is the source of truth
decisions.context: Nightshift needs deterministic machine-readable control data while humans still need readable documentation.
decisions.decision: All machine-read documents use JSON as their master representation. Human-readable documents are generated from JSON and are never authoritative.
consequences: Nightshift reads JSON only for project control data.
Generated human-readable documents can always be recreated.
When generated documentation differs from JSON, JSON prevails.
